author: nero_eth
content:
In order to cap the nr of deposits/block on the EL, we can have a hard cap that is < 8192 (what the CL limit is today) and >728 (the max deposits per tx with 2**24 gas) to avoid running into DoS issues.

E.g. hard cap it at 1028, worst-case deposits of a tx are statically inferable: (tx.gas_limit - intrinsic_gas) // 23k (cost of a deposit). With EIP-7825's 16.78M tx gas cap that's at most ~728 deposits per tx. Since the cap (1024) is above that, every single tx is always includable.
More here:
https://github.com/nerolation/hegota-eip-presentations/blob/main/deposit_cap_eip.md

edit: realized this is essentially what [eip-8254](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8254) does, but adds the heuristic to prevent builder dos.

So eip-8254 + adopting the O(1) packing/building rule that protects against dos should be done in glam.
cc <@808969530608451584> <@412614104222531604>

author: jih2nn
content: Just had a convo with <@792404665068158998>. IIUC, the O(1) packing/building rule is stopping building when you hit the deposit limit. For instance, if you hit the deposit limit with, let's say, 15M gas left, you still stop including more transaction. I think that's the main difference with 8254.

author: nero_eth
content:
right yeah. 8254 could implicitly assume that builders figure that out themselves, but at the same time clients have to equip their local builder software with this dos protection as well.
Everything consensus-related stays as-is in 8254. The only thing that would change is mentioning the packing heuristic.
